articles.title: ‘I have a $2,000 deductible’: I reported a cracked windshield on my Hertz rental car. Can they charge me?

articles.title: Trump looks set to roll out a new set of tariffs. Will markets care?
articles.description: The president is expected to impose fresh import taxes this week, as levies from February are only able to remain in effect for 150 days.

articles.title: Your healthcare could now cost $185,500 in retirement — and that doesn’t include long-term care
articles.description: Rising prices for care and managing chronic conditions helped drive up the forecast.

articles.title: Oil prices may fall, but gasoline prices won’t. Look at the trap we’re in.
articles.description: The Iran war isn’t causing your energy bill to soar. Blame Wall Street for that.

articles.title: It’s ‘incredibly disrespectful’ to do a virtual job interview by phone — but Gen Z is doing it anyway
articles.description: “This has more to do with the technology they grew up with rather than a sign of respect.”

articles.title: Here’s how to time your retirement to keep your healthcare costs down

articles.title: An oil lifeline is under threat, and markets have yet to price in the growing crisis

articles.description: Plus, investors just found out when insiders can start dumping their shares.

articles.title: These signs suggest investors should give the software sector a second chance
articles.description: Software stocks and loans have started to find their footing in July, suggesting it may be time for investors to forgive and forget.

articles.title: Import prices post surprise gain as costs of goods from China hit highest since 2008

articles.title: World Cup gave bars and restaurants a needed boost as consumers flash warning signs, Fed says
articles.description: The latest edition of the Fed's Beige Book indicated that the soccer tournament wasn't necessarily translating to broader economic growth.

articles.title: Wholesale prices unexpectedly declined 0.3% in June on big drop in gasoline

articles.title: India's inflation accelerates to 4.38% in June, exceeding forecasts

articles.title: Job seekers giving up: Labor force participation rate falls to lowest in 50 years, outside of Covid era

articles.title: U.S. job creation cools in June with payrolls growth of just 57,000; unemployment rate at 4.2%

articles.title: Europe wants to rebalance trade with Beijing, but can't quit Chinese air conditioners
